title: Logical Operators
content:
### Logical Operators

**Logical operators** combine boolean values to make complex decisions.

---

### The Operators

| Operator | Name | Description | Example |
|----------|------|-------------|---------|
| `&&` | AND | `true` if **both** are true | `true && false` = `false` |
| `||` | OR | `true` if **either** is true | `true || false` = `true` |
| `!` | NOT | **Inverts** the boolean | `!true` = `false` |

---

### Truth Table

| A | B | A && B | A `||` B | !A |
|---|---|--------|---------|----|
| `true` | `true` | `true` | `true` | `false` |
| `true` | `false` | `false` | `true` | `false` |
| `false` | `true` | `false` | `true` | `true` |
| `false` | `false` | `false` | `false` | `true` |

---

### Short-Circuit Evaluation

- `&&` stops evaluating if the **first** value is falsy
- `||` stops evaluating if the **first** value is truthy

```js
false && console.log("never runs");  // Short-circuits
true || console.log("never runs");   // Short-circuits
```

---

> **Practical Use:** `||` is often used to provide **default values**: `const name = input || "default"`.
